definition: ROIC for multinationals is systematically distorted by 200-400bp due to currency mismatch: NOPAT calculated at average exchange rates divided by invested capital at historical exchange rates. A 10% euro depreciation reduces NOPAT proportionally but leaves the denominator largely unchanged, making ROIC trend analysis unreliable without explicit currency normalization.
evidence: ~85% of multinationals with >30% international revenue report constant-currency metrics
SEC comment letters (2015, 2018, 2022) document asymmetric non-GAAP practices
open_questions: Do sophisticated investors already adjust for this or does the market misprice it (lower confidence 5.5/10)?
What is the aggregate impact on index-level ROIC during major FX moves?
Could standardized currency-normalized ROIC become a reporting requirement?
